citations.narrative:
The enclosure housing 4 African Crested porcupines does not provide adequate shelter for inclement weather. Located
within the enclosure along the back portion of the fence is a compile of wooden logs approximately 2 ft in length and half a
foot in thickness. Three have fallen off its base and the rest formed an approximate, 2ft by 2ft square with a height of
approximately 1 ft, making it difficult to house all 4 animals appropriately. The licensee states that this was serving as their
shelter, but that they tore it down. Licensee has a work order and plans to improve the enclosure with a roof.
Secured a long the top of the enclosure is a red tarp that is centered above the enclosure and takes up approximately
75% of the top. It is secured to each end of the enclosure but is being weighed down by a collection of water. Beneath the
tarp is a dry area of concrete corresponding to the area beneath the tarp, while the rest of the floor is wet. The dry portion
of the floor is approximately half a foot in distance from the torn down shelter.
Lack of adequate shelter can adversely affect the animal’s health and wellbeing. Stress, injury, and discomfort may occur
when exposed to harsh elements such as rain, snow, and wind. Natural or artificial shelter appropriate to the local climatic
conditions for the species concerned shall be provided for all animals kept outdoors to afford them protection and to
prevent discomfort to such animals.
Correct by October 6, 2025.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.127(b) 
   
Facilities, outdoor. 
The enclosure housing 4 African Crested porcupines does not provide adequate shelter for inclement weather. Located 
within the enclosure along the back portion of the fence is a compile of wooden logs approximately 2 ft in length and half a 
foot in thickness. Three have fallen off its base and the rest formed an approximate, 2ft by 2ft square with a height of 
approximately 1 ft, making it difficult to house all 4 animals appropriately. The licensee states that this was serving as their 
shelter, but that they tore it down. Licensee has a work order and plans to improve the enclosure with a roof. 
 
Secured a long the top of the enclosure is a red tarp that is centered above the enclosure and takes up approximately 
75% of the top. It is secured to each end of the enclosure but is being weighed down by a collection of water. Beneath the 
tarp is a dry area of concrete corresponding to the area beneath the tarp, while the rest of the floor is wet. The dry portion 
of the floor is approximately half a foot in distance from the torn down shelter. 
 
Lack of adequate shelter can adversely affect the animal’s health and wellbeing. Stress, injury, and discomfort may occur 
when exposed to harsh elements such as rain, snow, and wind. Natural or artificial shelter appropriate to the local climatic 
conditions for the species concerned shall be provided for all animals kept outdoors to afford them protection and to 
prevent discomfort to such animals.  
 
Correct by October 6, 2025. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
